title: ferray-fft: rfft doesn't use rustfft's real-to-complex FFT — promotes to complex first
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

rfft() in real.rs converts the real input to Complex<f64> via real_to_complex_flat(), runs a full complex FFT, then truncates to n/2+1 values. This does 2x the work of a real-to-complex FFT. rustfft doesn't have a dedicated real FFT, but the standard approach is to pack pairs of real values into the real/imaginary parts of n/2 complex values and use a complex FFT of half the size plus a post-processing step. NumPy's pocketfft has dedicated real FFT routines (rfft_n_even, rfft_n_odd) that exploit the symmetry. Alternative: use the realfft crate which provides RealFftPlanner on top of rustfft.
